helpful: Full provisioned petition and BIO text — the BIO's preservation argument was the single most decision-relevant input
statpack per-fee-class (paid vs IFP) and relist-count cuts
gaps: statpack.md in this build lacks the salience-band table the prompt describes; had to anchor on relist/CVSG/fee-class cuts instead
fedcourts query has no free-text similarity filter, so 'similar priors' for a niche legal question (judicial immunity) can't be pulled; generic era-ranked results skew to high-salience granted cases
a statpack cut for pro-se-within-paid petitions would sharpen the prior for exactly this case profile
notes: Single corpus query cost 429 ranged GETs / ~112 MB for 6 rows; fine for one call but worth knowing before iterating on filters.
